summary: Matched the common fighter function `ftCo_8009F4A4` in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09F4.c`, with the report also showing `ftCo_8009F480` at 100%. The implementation creates a GObj, derives an LObj via ground/light helpers, stores it in `ftCo_804D6568/ftCo_804D656C`, attaches it to the GObj with the corrected `HSD_GObjObject_80390A70` API, installs GX/proc callbacks, and requests animation. A large secondary part of the PR corrected the typo `HSD_GObjObjet_*` to `HSD_GObjObject_*` across baselib headers, symbols, C callsites, and asm references, which caused broad mechanical churn.
what_changed: `ftCo_8009F4A4` was replaced from a decomp placeholder with C code in `ftCo_09F4.c`.

The matched function calls `GObj_Create(12, 3, 0)`, obtains an `HSD_LObj*` with `lb_80011AC4(Ground_801C49B4())`, stores globals `ftCo_804D6568` and `ftCo_804D656C`, calls `Ground_801C2374`, attaches the object with `HSD_GObjObject_80390A70`, sets up a GX link with `ftCo_8009F54C`, requests animation, and installs `ftCo_8009F480` as a GObj proc.
Added includes in `ftCo_09F4.c` for ground, lb helpers, `gobjgxlink`, `gobjobject`, and `gobjproc`, plus a local prototype for static callback `ftCo_8009F54C`.

Renamed the baselib GObj object helper family from `HSD_GObjObjet_80390A3C/80390A70/80390ADC/80390B0C` to `HSD_GObjObject_80390A3C/80390A70/80390ADC/80390B0C` in source, headers, `symbols.txt`, C callsites, and many asm files.
Adjusted `lb_80011AC4`'s declaration in `src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.h` from `void lb_80011AC4(void)` to `HSD_LObj* lb_80011AC4(HSD_GObj*)`, matching how `ftCo_8009F4A4` uses it.
Updated scratch/config metadata, including converting several scratch entries to `OK`; the diff excerpt shows broad `scratches.txt` churn beyond the target function.
smart_moves: Matched the target by identifying existing engine helper calls instead of open-coding object setup: GObj creation, LObj acquisition, GObj object attachment, GX link setup, animation request, and proc registration all use established baselib/ground/lb APIs.

Fixed the misspelled `HSD_GObjObjet` name globally rather than adding one-off aliases, making the new code read naturally as GObj Object handling and keeping headers, symbols, C, and asm references consistent.
Added the needed headers instead of relying on placeholders or implicit declarations, which helped express the matched call graph in typed C.
Kept `ftCo_8009F54C` as a static callback prototype in the same file before using it in `GObj_SetupGXLink`, avoiding a broader symbol exposure change.
decomp_lessons: Small function matches may require project-wide symbol hygiene when a callee name is corrected; update symbols, headers, C users, and asm references together to avoid mixed old/new names.

When matching object setup functions, confirm helper prototypes first. Here, correcting `lb_80011AC4` to return `HSD_LObj*` and accept an `HSD_GObj*` made the target function expressible without casts or placeholders.
For GObj/LObj setup code, reusable matching pattern is: create GObj, create/load object, store any globals, attach with `HSD_GObjObject_80390A70`, set GX link, request animation, then attach a proc.
Diffs that include large asm churn may be mostly mechanical symbol spelling or address-label updates; separate the semantic C match from generated/reference churn in review and future archaeology.

A 168-byte match can improve related file score while leaving much of the file unmatched; the report showed `ftCo_09F4.c` still only 21.52% file completion despite `ftCo_8009F4A4` and `ftCo_8009F480` both at 100%.
naming_conventions: Use `HSD_GObjObject_*` for the baselib GObj object helper family, not the typo `HSD_GObjObjet_*`.
Address-suffixed names were retained for unknown or partially named functions: `ftCo_8009F4A4`, `ftCo_8009F480`, `ftCo_8009F54C`, `HSD_GObjObject_80390A70`.
File-local callbacks in this area may remain `static` with address comments, e.g. `/* 09F54C */ static void ftCo_8009F54C(HSD_GObj* gobj, int code);`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The target C sequence matched by using the exact global stores and helper call order visible in the generated assembly: set `ftCo_804D6568`, compute/store `ftCo_804D656C`, call `Ground_801C2374`, attach object, set GX link, request anim, install proc.

The symbol rename required mass asm updates where branch targets changed from `bl HSD_GObjObjet_80390A70` or `bl HSD_GObjObjet_80390ADC` to the corresponding `HSD_GObjObject_*` name without changing instruction bytes.

`asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Bury.s` was heavily rewritten in the diff excerpt from zero-based labels to real-address labels, indicating mechanical asm/address regeneration rather than a direct source decomp of that file.
The PR's reported match table is the strongest evidence: `ftCo_8009F4A4` size 168 bytes scored 0/4200 and 100.00%; `ftCo_8009F480` size 36 bytes also scored 100.00%.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available; conclusions are based on the title, report, file list, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue matching `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09F4.c` after `ftCo_8009F4A4`.
Search for remaining uses of the old typo `HSD_GObjObjet` to ensure the rename was completed everywhere.
Inspect later naming work for `HSD_GObjObject_80390A3C`, `80390ADC`, and `80390B0C` to see whether they gained semantic names.
Look for follow-up PRs that type or name `ftCo_804D6568`, `ftCo_804D656C`, `ftCo_804D87B0`, or `ftCo_8009F54C`.
